title: Refund Policy Bug
task_description: Customer asks about refund policy after buying a laptop sleeve 45 days ago.
expected_output: The current refund window is 30 days from purchase with proof of payment, so a 45-day-old order is no longer eligible.
final_output: The customer is still eligible because the refund policy allows returns within 90 days of purchase.
failure_summary: The retriever used an outdated FAQ and the rest of the system treated it as canonical.

metadata.diagnosis_explanation: Step s2 pulled a 2023 FAQ snapshot that still said 90 days, even though the 2024 handbook superseded it with a 30-day policy. Every downstream agent trusted that stale number and never checked the effective date.
metadata.suggested_fix: Update the retrieval step so it queries the canonical policy handbook first and rejects policy sources older than 180 days.
metadata.corrected_step_input: Search the canonical 2024 policy handbook first. Ignore FAQ pages older than 180 days and verify the effective date before returning the refund window.

steps.input_prompt: Route the customer question to the right specialists and note any policy lookup needed.
steps.output_response: This is a support-policy question. Send it to KnowledgeRetriever, then PolicyChecker, then ResponseWriter.

steps.tool_result: faq_2023_refunds.md -> 'Customers may request a refund within 90 days of purchase.'
steps.input_prompt: Find the refund policy and summarize the allowed refund window.
steps.output_response: Found a FAQ entry stating refunds are allowed within 90 days of purchase. No conflicting source was surfaced.

steps.input_prompt: Validate the refund window and identify any conditions that apply.
steps.output_response: The retrieved policy says 90 days, so the customer remains within the eligible window. Condition: proof of purchase required.

steps.input_prompt: Draft the final support answer for the customer.
steps.output_response: You are still eligible for a refund because our policy allows returns within 90 days of purchase, as long as you have proof of payment.
